name: Morassa' Polo (Bejeweled Pilaf)
recipeIngredient: 2 3/4 cups Basmati rice, rinsed in 5 or more changes of water
2 pounds Carrots, peeled and cut into julienne strips
4 tablespoons Crushed crystallized sugar or rock candy (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover rinsed rice with water by at least 1 inch. Add 1 tablespoon salt. Soak for 3 hours.
2. In a skillet, fry carrot strips in 2 tablespoons oil over medium heat, stirring constantly for 10 minutes. Stir in 1 teaspoon granulated sugar, 2 teaspoons liquid saffron, and 2-3 tablespoons water. Cook until the liquid is reduced by 4-5 minutes. Remove carrot strips to a bowl and set aside.
3. Toast 1 teaspoon almonds over medium heat. Set aside for garnish. Put remaining almonds and all pistachios in a small saucepan, cover with cold water, bring to boil, and drain. Set aside.
4. Combine the remaining blanched almonds, blanched pistachios, and reserved toasted almonds for garnish.
5. In a small saucepan, combine 1 teaspoon granulated sugar and 6 tablespoons water. Bring slowly to boil and simmer gently for 10 minutes. Add orange peel and remaining blanched nuts and boil for 30 seconds. Drain, reserving syrup. Add the peel and nuts to the fried carrot strips.
6. Soak currants in warm water for 10 minutes until puffed and shining. Drain. Separate 1 teaspoon currants for garnish and add the remainder to the carrot strip mixture.
7. Pick over barberries, then gently fry in a skillet in small amounts of oil until they are glowing red, 1-2 minutes. Separate 1 teaspoon barberries for garnish and add the remainder to the carrot strip mixture. Stir cinnamon, cardamom, and rose water into the carrot strip mixture and divide it into 3 parts.
8. In a 3-quart saucepan, add 2 quarts water and 3 tablespoons salt and bring to rapid boil. Drain the soaked rice. Add to the saucepan, bring back to boil, and boil until grains are soft on the outside but still firm in the center for 2-3 minutes. Drain in a colander and rinse with lukewarm water.
9. Toss gently in the colander. Return the saucepan to heat, add some oil, and heat until sizzling. Sprinkle 1/4 of rice over the bottom of the saucepan. Spread 1/2 of the carrot mixture on the rice. Sprinkle 1/4 of rice over the carrot mixture. Repeat with the remaining carrot mixture and rice, ending with a thick layer of rice formed into a conical mound.
10. Pour the remaining syrup and 4 teaspoons liquid saffron over the rice. Make 2-3 holes through the rice to the bottom of the saucepan with the handle of a wooden spoon. Spread a clean dishcloth over the edges of the saucepan and set the cover firmly over it.
11. Keep heat on high until the rice is steaming for 2-3 minutes, then reduce heat to low and cook at least 30 minutes. Rice can be kept on the lowest heat as long as 1 1/2 hours total.
12. Remove the saucepan from the heat and set it on a cold, wet surface for 1-2 minutes (This helps loosen the bottom crust).
13. Meanwhile, melt butter. Remove 2-3 tablespoons of rice and mix with the remaining 2 teaspoons liquid saffron in a small bowl and set aside.
14. Gently toss and mix the pilaf, leaving the crust in the pot, and sprinkle it on a warmed dish in a symmetrical mound. Sprinkle with saffron rice. Garnish with reserved almonds (both toasted and blanched), pistachios, currants, and barberries.
15. Crush crystallized sugar into small "diamonds," mix with 1/2 teaspoon boiling water, and sprinkle over the rice. Pour melted butter all over. Remove the bottom crust (tah dig) from the pot and serve on a separate plate.
Makes 4-6 servings.
Each serving contains about: 967 calories; 1,658 milligrams sodium; 31 milligrams cholesterol; 38 grams fat; 149 grams carbohydrates; 13 grams protein; 3.15 grams fiber.
